mission.title: metallb: Existing services with more than two external IPs prevent MetalLB from completing setup
mission.description: Existing services with more than two external IPs prevent MetalLB from completing setup. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current metallb deployment
steps.description:
Verify your MetalLB deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n metallb-system
helm list -n metallb-system 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This issue affects MetalLB deployments where pre-existing services have multiple external IPs.

steps.title: Review metallb configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant MetalLB configuration and services:
```bash
kubectl get svc -A -o wide | grep LoadBalancer
kubectl get ipaddresspools.metallb.io -n metallb-system
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metallb -n metallb-system --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for services with multiple external IPs that may prevent MetalLB from completing setup. Check for error messages like `Failed to retrieve lbIPs family`.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Existing services with more than two external IPs prevent…
steps.description:
The fix handles services with malformed load balancer IPs gracefully. When MetalLB encounters a service with invalid LB IPs and cannot determine the IP family, instead of erroring out and reprocessing in a loop, it now clears the invalid status and lets the assignment logic add a new IP.

Example MetalLB configuration:
```yaml
apiVersion: metallb.io/v1beta1
kind: IPAddressPool
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: metallb-system
spec:
  addresses:
  - 192.168.1.180-192.168.1.189
---
apiVersion: metallb.io/v1beta1
kind: L2Advertisement
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: metallb-system
spec:
  ipAddressPools:
  - default
```

See the fix PR for details: https://github.com/metallb/metallb/pull/1778

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n metallb-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metallb
kubectl get events -n metallb-system --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Existing services with more than two external IPs prevent…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Currently, when metallb receives a service with malformed lb ips and it's not able to determine the ip family, it errors out and reprocesses the service.

Here if the lb part of the status is invalid we just wipe it and let the assignement logic add a new ip. This prevents malformed services to cause infinite loops and helps in recovering the service.
codeSnippets:
NAMESPACE              NAME                              TYPE           CLUSTER-IP      EXTERNAL-IP                                 PORT(S)                      AGE
default                kubernetes                        ClusterIP      10.43.0.1       <none>                                      443/TCP                      259d
kube-system            kube-dns                          ClusterIP      10.43.0.10      <none>                                      53/UDP,53/TCP,9153/TCP       259d
kube-system            metrics-server                    ClusterIP      10.43.52.43     <none>                                      443/TCP                      259d
kube-system            traefik                           LoadBalancer   10.43.210.150   192.168.1.170,192.168.1.171,192.168.1.172   80:32

---
apiVersion: metallb.io/v1beta1
kind: IPAddressPool
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: metallb-system
spec:
  addresses:
  - 192.168.1.180-192.168.1.189

---
apiVersion: metallb.io/v1beta1
kind: L2Advertisement
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: metallb-system
spec:
  ipAddressPools:
  - default

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with metallb installed or the issue environment reproducible.
